claim: Replay uses the same platform profile and byte-identical firmware image identity as the recorded run.
current_limit: Identity fields are specified and generated firmware artifacts exist, but there is no external compiler or full RTL replay identity check.

theorem_assumption: A1/A3 deterministic architectural state and core step
claim: For equal platform, firmware, state, and boundary input, the replay model produces the same next architectural state and observation.
current_limit: The local interpreter and model are deterministic for the six workloads; this is not a mechanized RV32I ISA proof.

claim: Replay capsules retain every nondeterministic boundary event needed by the scoped benchmark failures.
current_limit: Completeness is exercised for six model benchmarks and twelve RTL-smoke capsules, not for arbitrary firmware or full benchmark-wide RTL traces.

claim: Replay comparison rejects missing, duplicate, shifted cycle/commit indices, and same-index reordered events.
current_limit: Comparator/order checks are generated cycle-index and commit-index fixtures plus RTL-smoke export checks; full firmware-running RTL replay remains pending.

claim: Interrupt timing is represented at commit-index boundaries and reproduced in seeded RTL-smoke schedules.
current_limit: Seeded interrupt evidence covers RTL-smoke interrupt-race schedules and seed-specific corruption rejection, while stronger randomized record/replay cases remain TODO; there is no full interrupt-controller proof or benchmark-wide randomized campaign.

claim: MMIO read values, write observations, and corruption of MMIO payloads are replay-visible and checked.
current_limit: Current checks cover local logger/control contracts and benchmark fixtures, not a complete bus-protocol proof.

claim: External architectural mutations must be captured as ordered boundary events when present.
current_limit: The current six workloads do not include DMA or external RAM mutation RTL tests; the contract is specified but not exercised.

claim: The property checker emits deterministic failure IDs/signatures over equal trace observations.
current_limit: Checks cover local property/signature RTL contracts and smoke alignment, not every future safety property.

claim: Capsules and replay comparisons target the finite prefix through the selected property failure.
current_limit: Prefix behavior is checked for model/firmware-sim workloads and bounded recorder contracts, not a full processor/replay theorem.

theorem_assumption: A11 recorder noninterference separate from sufficiency theorem
claim: The theorem applies to the recorded execution and does not itself prove instrumentation noninterference.
current_limit: No perturbation or runtime-overhead proof exists yet; mapped and benchmark-wide hardware results are pending.
